description: Direct articulation of specific product benefits with practical usage instructions and visible results, emphasizing how the product solves a beauty concern or enhances appearance.
structural_recipe: Open with a problem statement or desire (e.g., 'سر للحصول على'). Present the product name clearly. Explain the primary benefit using sensory language (لمعان، نعومة، إشراق). Add secondary benefits or context about how it works. Include a call-to-action verb (استخدمي، اكتشفي). Close with relevant hashtags mixing Arabic brand language with English beauty terms.
why_it_works: Saudi audiences respond strongly to educational beauty content that demystifies skincare routines and positions products as solutions to specific concerns. The combination of practical advice ('استخدمي بانتظام') with tangible results ('لمعان ونعومة') creates trust and aspiration. Arabic language builds authenticity while English hashtags signal professionalism and international quality standards.
transplantation_caveats: Ensure product claims are substantiated to comply with Saudi consumer protection standards
Avoid overpromising results; use measured language like 'يُعزز' (enhances) rather than absolute claims
Include hashtags in both Arabic and English to maximize reach across bilingual Saudi audiences
